title:
Basement
Sessions
subtitle: LATE NIGHT MUSIC
description: Concrete walls sweating bass. A borrowed amp, two decks and a single red bulb. Nobody remembers who brought the smoke machine.

title:
Friday
Nights
subtitle: BAD DECISIONS
description: 3000 people and one fire exit. Sweat on the ceiling, a kick drum you felt in your teeth, and a sunrise nobody planned for.

title:
City
Lights
subtitle: LATE NIGHTS
description: Rain on the lens, neon bleeding into the gutter. A whole city pretending it wasn't watching us walk home.

title:
Skate
or Die
subtitle: CONCRETE GODS
description: An empty pool, a borrowed deck, and a security guard who pretended not to see. Scarred shins, perfect grip tape.

title:
Family
First
subtitle: SUNDAY KITCHENS
description: Plastic tablecloth, a camcorder nobody knew how to use, and a grandmother who narrated every frame. The realest archive of all.

title:
Cassette
Culture
subtitle: 120 MINUTES
description: Hand-drawn J-cards and a pause button worn smooth. The mixtape was the message; the hiss was the warmth.

title:
Road
Trip
subtitle: NO MAP
description: A full tank, a dead radio, and a horizon that kept lying about how close it was. We slept in the car and called it freedom.

title:
Block
Party
subtitle: HOT ASPHALT
description: Speakers in a window, a hydrant cracked open, and every kid on the block convinced they invented summer.

title:
Trainyard
Graffiti
subtitle: FRESH BURNERS
description: Cans rattling in a backpack, a lookout on the fence, and a piece that lived exactly one morning before the buff.

title:
Beach
Days
subtitle: SALT & GRAIN
description: Disposable camera crusted with salt, a cooler full of warm soda, and a sunburn we'd feel for a week.

title:
After
Hours
subtitle: STARTS LATE
description: The real party started when the lights came up everywhere else. No sign, no flyer, just a number and a knock.

title:
First
Car
subtitle: RUSTED WHEELS
description: Three hundred dollars, a tape deck that ate everything, and freedom that smelled like motor oil and pine air freshener.

title:
The Room
Becomes Evidence
subtitle: OCTOBER 26, 1998. XAVIER ENTERS THE ROOM.
description: Xavier entered the room with the confidence of a man who had misunderstood both the dress code and the decade. Within minutes, witnesses reported smoke, decorative stars, and the strong impression that paperwork would follow.
